title: rubus fuscoater subsp. oigocladus
side_effects: Rubus fuscoater subsp. oigocladus can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, including hives, itching, and difficulty breathing in some individuals. Ingesting the plant can cause gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, and may also lead to liver and kidney damage in severe cases. Additionally, topical application of the plant's extract can cause skin irritation, redness, and inflammation, particularly if left on the skin for extended periods. If not handled and prepared correctly, Rubus fuscoater subsp. oigocladus can also interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications, potentially exacerbating underlying health conditions.
